title: TC001-Dashboard loads current-month overview after successful OTP verification
description: Verifies that completing the OTP sign-in flow lands the user on the dashboard and shows current-month summary and chart content.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to the dashboard after successful OTP sign-in"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Income vs Expense')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The dashboard should show current-month income, expense, and balance summaries and an income-vs-expense chart after signing in"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

The OTP sign-in flow is not available on the /auth page, so the OTP verification and dashboard landing could not be tested.

Observations:
- The auth page displays email and password input fields and a 'Sign In' button.
- There is no 'Request OTP' or 'Send OTP' control and no separate OTP input step.
- Unable to proceed with OTP verification because the UI lacks OTP-related controls.

title: TC002-Dashboard is gated behind OTP login
description: Verifies that an unauthenticated user attempting to access the dashboard is prompted to authenticate before the dashboard is accessible.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /dashboard and verify that the authentication flow is shown instead of the dashboard overview (i.e., unauthenticated access is prompted to sign in).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/dashboard")
        
        # -> Click the 'Reload' button to retry loading the page and then check whether the authentication page or the dashboard is shown. If the site still fails to respond, report the test as blocked.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the Reload button to retry loading the page and then re-check whether the authentication page or the dashboard is shown.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Sign in')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The authentication page should prompt the user to sign in before accessing the dashboard."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The feature could not be reached — the application server did not respond, preventing the test from verifying the authentication flow.

Observations:
- The browser shows 'This page isn’t working' with ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE for localhost.
- Clicking the Reload button did not recover the site (Reload control is present).
- Direct navigation to /dashboard returned no response (ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE).

title: TC003-Dashboard is gated behind OTP login for transactions ledger
description: Verifies that an unauthenticated user attempting to access the transactions page is prompted to authenticate before the ledger is accessible.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /dashboard/transactions and confirm that the authentication flow is shown instead of the transactions ledger for an unauthenticated user.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/dashboard/transactions")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Sign in')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The authentication page should prompt the user to sign in before accessing the transactions ledger"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The feature could not be reached — the web server on localhost is not responding so the test cannot proceed.

Observations:
- Navigating to the app shows an ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE page: "localhost didn't send any data."
- The browser displays an empty response error page with a Reload button, preventing access to /auth or /dashboard/transactions.

title: TC004-Dashboard shows current-month summary and chart by default
description: Verifies that a signed-in user viewing the dashboard sees the default current-month summary cards and analyzer chart.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Fill the email and password fields and click the Sign In button to load the dashboard.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('finefinanceservice@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('9842884614')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Submit the sign-in form to load the dashboard (press Enter from the password field) and wait for the dashboard to load.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Navigate to /dashboard and verify whether the dashboard loads. If it loads, check for current-month income, expense, and balance summary cards and an income-vs-expense analyzer chart. Then finish the test.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/dashboard")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Current month')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The dashboard should show current-month income, expense, and balance summary cards after sign-in"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Income vs Expense')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The dashboard should display an income-vs-expense chart for the current month after sign-in"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The dashboard could not be reached because the local server did not respond.

Observations:
- The page shows 'This page isn’t working' with 'ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE'.
- No dashboard content is present; only a 'Reload' button is available.

title: TC005-Create a transaction and see it appear in the ledger
description: Verifies that creating a new transaction with category, type, amount, and date saves successfully and appears in the transaction ledger.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Fill the email field, fill the password field, and click Sign In to authenticate.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('finefinanceservice@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('9842884614')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Try submitting the sign-in form again by focusing the password field and sending Enter to trigger form submission.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., '123.45')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The new transaction with amount 123.45 should appear in the ledger after saving."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The test could not proceed because sign-in could not be completed from the current page.

Observations:
- The /auth page shows email and password fields filled, but no interactive Sign In button is exposed.
- Submitting via Enter was attempted twice and the page remained on /auth with no navigation or OTP prompt.
- Without a way to successfully submit credentials or receive an OTP, the transaction creation step cannot be reached.

title: TC012-Incorrect or expired OTP shows a retry error
description: Verifies that submitting an invalid OTP shows a validation error and keeps the user in the authentication flow.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Invalid OTP')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "An OTP validation error should be visible after submitting an invalid OTP"
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/auth' in current_url, "The page should have remained in the authentication flow after submitting an invalid OTP"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The OTP authentication flow is not available on the /auth page, so I could not perform the invalid OTP submission test.

Observations:

- There is no OTP input field or 'Request OTP' button present on the page.

title: TC016-Client-side validation blocks invalid transaction creation
description: Verifies that leaving required fields blank or entering an invalid amount shows client-side validation errors and prevents saving.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Fill the email field with the provided username, fill the password, and submit the form (send Enter) to attempt login.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('finefinanceservice@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('9842884614')
        
        # -> Click the Sign In button on the auth page to submit the credentials and attempt login.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Please enter a valid amount')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The form should display 'Please enter a valid amount' when a negative amount is entered.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'No transactions found')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The ledger should show 'No transactions found' after attempting to save an invalid transaction.",
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

TEST BLOCKED

The sign-in form cannot be submitted from the auth page, preventing access to the application and the transactions feature required for the validation tests.
